title: The Labourers in the Vineyard
subtitle: The householder goes out at every hour, and the wage is a single coin: eternal life

stillness: Quiet now, and consider one thing.
Idleness is the failure to do the work you owe.
Sit in this hour, whichever hour it is, and listen.
gospel: Many that are first shall be last,
and the last shall be first.
The kingdom of heaven is like to a householder,
who went out early in the morning
to hire labourers into his vineyard.
And having agreed with the labourers for a penny a day,
he sent them into his vineyard.
And going out about the third hour,
he saw others standing in the marketplace, idle,
Go you also into my vineyard,
and I will give you what shall be just.
behold: See Judas, the Apostle, turned into an apostate;
and see the thief upon the cross made a confessor.
See the householder go out, early in the morning,
not because he was hidden, but to make himself known;
while he is not known, he is in secret,
and the more often he goes out, the more widely he gives himself to be known.
Look upon the marketplace: the world,
where slanders, injuries, and quarrels never cease,
and all things are for sale.
In this marketplace the souls of men are set out for sale.
There are two merchants: God and the devil.
consider: The householder is God the Father —
called man not by nature of substance, but by the affection of his tenderness,
for he is humane: kind, gentle, and merciful toward men.
The labourers are the preachers, and all who live rightly;
but many today are only word-makers, not workers,
who say, and do not do.
The penny is eternal life, promised to the Lord's labourers.
It is called a penny by its name, for it was worth ten coins,
the wage promised for keeping the ten of the Decalogue.
It is round in shape, where no beginning and no end can be found:
in this is signified the eternity of life, the full possession of eternity.
Upon it is stamped the image of the king:
so the image of God is pressed into the blessed, transforming them into himself.
Some men are so blind that they sell their own soul to the devil for a cheap price —
for a little pleasure of this present life, like the greedy and the lustful;
some for the honours and glory of the world, like the proud;
some for riches and temporal goods, like the robbers and the covetous.
Let us flee that merchant, and sell our souls to Christ,
who bought us with his precious blood.
Idleness is the failure to do the work you owe.
Sinners are dead, not idle.
He who serves the devil is dead; he who does not do God's work is idle.
He who takes what is another's is dead; he who does not give his own is idle.
If you fast and give to no one, you are idle.
Adam was first placed in paradise, but because he did not till it, he was cast out;
and we too are placed here — and if we neglect it, we shall likewise be thrown out.
The ruin of those before us should be a warning to those who follow.
prayer: Lord Jesus Christ, most high householder,
you led me into your vineyard early in the morning,
when from my youth you mercifully called me to faith and to your service,
and agreed with me to labour for the penny of eternal life;
but I, wretched man, stood idle the whole day of my life,
and by living negligently did not do the work I owed.
Lord, because you are good and merciful,
grant that I may come to my senses at least in the eleventh hour,
and bring forth worthy fruits of penance,
that I may deserve to find with you some wage, however small. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: In isto foro, animæ hominum venales exponuntur. Mercatores duo sunt: Deus et diabolus.
latinJewel.english: In this marketplace the souls of men are set out for sale. There are two merchants: God and the devil.
